message:
fix(raptorq): unify max object size validation with byte-based 256-block contract

The RaptorQ sender previously validated max object size as
max_block_size * symbol_size, which is too restrictive when symbol_size
is small — an object that fits within 256 source blocks (the actual u8
SBN limit) would be rejected because the product is small.

The fix:
- New max_object_size(max_block_size) in encoding.rs: computes
  max_block_size * 256 (MAX_SOURCE_BLOCKS), matching the actual byte
  contract enforced by EncodingPipeline
- RaptorQSender::send_object() uses max_object_size() instead of the
  block_size * symbol_size formula
- Both test files updated to use max_object_size() for consistency

New tests:
- send_object_accepts_valid_non_default_small_symbol_size: 257 bytes
  with symbol_size=8, max_block_size=32 — fits 256 blocks, previously
  rejected
- send_object_rejects_large_symbol_size_payload: exceeds byte contract
- sender_allows_small_symbol_size_payloads_that_fit_byte_limit (builder)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
